architecture_plan.title: MAHL: Multi-Agent LLM-Guided Hierarchical Chiplet Design with Adaptive Debugging - Demo Implementation Architecture
architecture_plan.overview: A modular architecture for demonstrating the MAHL framework that enables multi-agent LLM-guided hierarchical chiplet design with adaptive debugging. The system implements hierarchical description generation, RTL implementation and validation, and design space exploration using LLMs to guide chiplet design processes.

modules.purpose: Coordinates multiple LLM agents to guide the chiplet design process, managing agent interactions and task distribution for hierarchical design generation
Generates hierarchical chiplet descriptions using LLMs, implementing the hierarchical description generation mechanism described in the paper
Handles RTL code generation and implementation based on hierarchical descriptions, integrating with validation components
Performs design space exploration to find optimal chiplet configurations, utilizing LLM guidance for efficient search
Implements adaptive debugging mechanisms that work alongside LLMs to identify and resolve design issues in real-time
Provides RTL validation and verification capabilities for generated chiplet designs
Manages communication with LLM services, handling prompts, responses, and agent coordination for design guidance
Stores and manages chiplet IP libraries, design templates, and reusable components for the design process

framework_suggestions: Pydantic for data validation and configuration management
LangChain for LLM integration and agent management
FastAPI for REST API endpoints for system interaction
Docker for containerization of LLM services and components
Celery for distributed task execution across agents
SQLAlchemy for database management of chiplet designs and repositories
